articles.title: Bafta Awards and a Shouted Word
articles.summary: At the Bafta film awards in February, a man named John Davidson, who has Tourette's syndrome, shouted a bad word that hurt people. This word was a racial slur, which is a mean word about someone's race. Two black actors, Michael B Jordan and Delroy Lindo, were on stage when it happened. Bafta, the group that runs the awards, said they did not do enough to protect guests, members, and viewers. They apologized to the black community, disabled community, and everyone watching. An independent review found that Bafta's planning was weak, but there was no bad intent. Bafta is now working to make things better so this doesn't happen again. The BBC also said the broadcast should not have aired the word.

articles.title: Katseye's New Music Video Without Manon
articles.summary: Katseye is a girl group that started in 2023 on a TV show called Dream Academy. They just released a music video for their song Pinky Up, but one member, Manon, is not in it. Manon said she is taking a break to take care of herself, and the group supports her. The video is colorful and has a techno-pop sound. Katseye is getting ready to perform at a big music festival called Coachella in California. Fans are sad that Manon is missing and want her back, but the group says they will keep performing without her for now. The video got over six million views in less than a day, showing many people like it.

articles.title: Labrinth Leaves Euphoria Music Team
articles.summary: Labrinth, a singer and producer from London, will not make music for the third season of the TV show Euphoria. He created the dramatic score and songs for the first two seasons, which are popular with teens. Last year, it was announced he would return with another composer, Hans Zimmer, but in March, Labrinth posted on Instagram that he was leaving and criticized HBO and Columbia. Now, reports confirm he is not involved in the new season coming out this weekend. Labrinth is still set to perform at Coachella music festival. The show's creator, Sam Levinson, said he doesn't know why Labrinth left but praised his work. Hans Zimmer, who makes music for movies, will help with the new season's score.

articles.title: Music in Space: Songs for Astronauts
articles.summary: Astronauts listen to music in space to feel happy and connected to Earth. On a recent trip around the moon, the Artemis II crew woke up to songs by artists like Chappell Roan and Denzel Curry. Denzel Curry was excited that his song was played in space and wants to be the first rapper to perform there. Another astronaut, Tim Peake, picked songs by Queen, U2, and Coldplay before going to the International Space Station in 2015. He says music is important because it reminds him of home, family, and friends. Hearing music in space feels strange but helps astronauts feel less lonely while looking at Earth from far away. Music makes space travel more fun and comforting.

articles.title: A Man's Expensive Coat and Tariff Trouble
articles.summary: Alex bought a coat from France, but he got a big surprise bill for extra fees called tariffs because the coat was made in Myanmar. The Supreme Court said these tariffs were not fair and ordered refunds. However, Alex might not get his money back because he paid through a shipping company, not directly. Many people like Alex are worried they won't get refunds even though the court ruled in their favor. The government is setting up a system to give money back, but it's tricky because some costs were already passed on to customers through higher prices. This story shows how tariffs can affect everyday shoppers.

articles.title: Young Tennis Star Helps Great Britain Win Big Match
articles.summary: Mika Stojsavljevic, a 17-year-old tennis player from Great Britain, won an exciting match against Talia Gibson from Australia. Mika is ranked much lower than Talia but played bravely and won in straight sets. This was her first time playing in the Billie Jean King Cup, a team competition. Later, Harriet Dart, another British player, also won her match against Kimberly Birrell after losing the first set. Great Britain now leads 2-0 and needs just one more win to advance to the finals in China. Mika was happy and thanked her supporters. Both players showed great skill and determination in their games.
